original_hypothesis.title: Watchers verifyRemoveLiquidity Is Empty No-Op, Bypassing Withdrawal Verification
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Watchers.verifyRemoveLiquidity is an empty function that performs no verification. BaseConnector.sendTokensToTrustedAddress relies on this function to verify the amount of tokens the AccountingManager is allowed to withdraw from connectors. A compromised manager/keeper can extract arbitrary amounts from connectors without any watcher-level validation.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The Watcher role is described in documentation as a monitoring role capable of 'undoing actions if misbehavior is detected.' The empty verification function eliminates this safeguard entirely, making the withdrawal path fully dependent on manager honesty.

proof_of_signal.pattern: function verifyRemoveLiquidity(uint256 withdrawAmount, uint256 sentAmount, bytes memory data) external view { } — completely empty function body

preconditions: Attacker has manager-level access (via compromised keys or flashLoan bug)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: No calculation or validation performed in verification function
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Compromised manager calls retrieveTokensForWithdraw with arbitrary amounts → sendTokensToTrustedAddress → Watcher check is no-op → tokens extracted
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Full extraction of connector-held assets without any programmatic safeguard

economic_questions: Is the Watcher verification intended to be implemented off-chain via the multisig?
What percentage of vault assets are held in connectors vs AccountingManager?
Would a Keeper multisig compromise bypass all other safeguards?
validation_requirements: Confirm verifyRemoveLiquidity is called in the withdrawal path
Test full extraction path with empty verification

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Trace sendTokensToTrustedAddress flow with AccountingManager as caller

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
